title: Malawians repatriated from South Africa amid xenophobia concerns

title: Kenya's ex-chief justice arrested at protest against building on national park

originalTitle: Somali referee Artan barred from entering USA
title: Omar's World Cup Dream: A Big Bump in the Road!
content:
Hello, Super Sports Fans!

Welcome, young sports enthusiasts! I'm your friendly African news reporter. Today, we have a story about a very special person. It's about a man who loves football so much. He worked very hard to follow his dream. Sometimes, things don't go exactly as we plan. This story is a bit sad, but it also teaches us about trying our best. Let's find out what happened!

Meet Omar Artan – A Super Referee!

Our story is about a kind man named Omar Artan. Omar comes from a country called Somalia in Africa. Omar is not a player who scores goals. He is a very important person on the football field. He is a referee! He helps make sure the game is fair for everyone. He wears a special uniform. He has a whistle!

What is a Referee, Anyway?

A referee is like the judge of a football game. They make sure all the players follow the rules. If a player does something wrong, the referee blows their whistle. They might show a yellow card or a red card. It's a very important job! Referees have to be very good at seeing things quickly. They need to know all the rules by heart. It's a lot of training and practice to be a good referee. Did you know? There are usually three referees on the field during a big football match! One main referee and two helpers on the sides. They all work together!

The Amazing World Cup!

Have you heard of the World Cup? It's the biggest football party in the whole world! Teams from many, many countries play against each other. They want to win a very shiny trophy. The World Cup happens only every four years. It's a very exciting time for football fans everywhere! Everyone cheers for their favourite team. It's like a huge festival of football! Did you know? The World Cup is watched by billions of people! That's more than half the people on Earth. It's super popular!

Omar's Big Dream!

Omar Artan had a huge dream. He wanted to be a referee at the World Cup! And guess what? He was almost there! Omar was going to be the very first person from Somalia to be a referee at the World Cup finals. Imagine how proud he, his family, and his whole country would feel! It was a dream come true for him. He worked for many years for this moment. He trained hard. He learned all the rules. He showed great skill and fairness in every game he refereed. This was his big chance to shine on the world stage.

Somalia – Omar's Home!

Omar comes from Somalia. Somalia is a country in East Africa. It has a very long coastline. That means it has beautiful beaches! Many people in Somalia love football. It's a very popular sport there. Just like many other countries in Africa, football brings people together in Somalia. They love to watch games and cheer for their teams. Omar would have made his whole country feel very proud by representing them at the World Cup. Did you know? Somalia is sometimes called the 'Horn of Africa' because of its shape on the map!

A Sad Surprise!

But then, something sad happened. Omar was on the list to be a referee for the World Cup. He was going to travel to a country called the United States. This is where some important World Cup matches will be played. But he was stopped. He was not allowed to enter the United States. Because he could not go to the United States, he could not be a referee at the World Cup. His name was taken off the list. It was a very unexpected turn of events for Omar and everyone who supported him.

What Does 'Denied Entry' Mean?

When you travel to another country, you sometimes need special permission. This permission is like a pass. It's called a visa. If a country says 'no' to your entry, it means you cannot go into that country. We don't know exactly why Omar was denied entry. Sometimes, there are many rules for people to enter a country. But it meant he could not travel there. This means he couldn't do his very important job at the World Cup.

The United States of America!

The United States of America is a very big country. It is far away from Africa. It has many big cities and tall buildings. It's a place where many big events happen, like sports tournaments and concerts. Sometimes, World Cup matches or other important football events happen there. It's a country known for many things, including big sports. Many different kinds of people live in the USA. Did you know? The United States is often called the USA for short! It has 50 different states, all with their own special things!

How Omar Might Feel

This news must have made Omar very sad. Imagine working so hard for a dream. Imagine being so close. And then, something unexpected happens. He must feel disappointed. It's okay to feel sad when things don't work out. It's normal to feel a bit down. But we know Omar is strong. He loves football. He will keep going. He will keep trying his best. We can all send him happy thoughts and encouragement.

Keeping Dreams Alive!

Even though Omar cannot be a referee at this World Cup, his journey is not over. He is still a wonderful referee. He can inspire many young children in Somalia and all over Africa. He showed everyone that with hard work, you can get very, very close to your dreams. Sometimes, we face challenges. But the most important thing is to never give up. We keep learning, we keep trying. Every time we face a challenge, we learn something new and become stronger. Did you know? Many famous football players faced challenges when they were young, like injuries or not being chosen for a team, but they never stopped playing and training! They kept their dreams alive.

Football Brings Us Together!

Football is more than just a game. It brings people from different countries together. It teaches us about teamwork and fair play. Even when there are sad moments, football reminds us that we are all part of one big family. We can all cheer for each other. We can all support each other's dreams. This sport has a special power to connect everyone, no matter where they are from. We learn to celebrate wins and also support each other during losses or disappointments.

Why This Matters

Omar Artan's story is important because it shows us that even amazing people face tough times. It reminds us to be kind and to keep trying. His dream of being the first Somali referee at the World Cup finals was so close. Even though it didn't happen this time, his effort and hard work are still amazing. He is still a hero to many! He teaches us to work hard for our goals. We hope he gets another chance very soon to show his great referee skills to the whole world. Go, Omar, go!
summary: Omar Artan, a referee from Somalia, had his dream of being the first Somalian to officiate at the World Cup finals put on hold after he was not allowed to enter the United States.
whyMatters: This story is important because it teaches us that even when we work super hard for a dream, unexpected things can happen, but we should always keep trying and never give up. Omar's hard work inspires us all!

originalTitle: Somali referee Artan to miss World Cup after being barred from entering US
title: Somali referee Artan to miss World Cup after being barred from entering US
content: Omar Artan, who was set to be the first Somalian to referee at the World Cup finals, is dropped from the list of officials after he was denied entry to the United States.

title: Why Olise can be one of France's very best - Clichy
